ActionCategories.Title: Network Issues
ActionCategories.Description: Fix common internet and network problems

Actions.Name: Fix Internet Connection
Actions.Description: Clear DNS cache and reset network
Actions.Script: Clear-DnsClientCache; Write-Output 'DNS cache cleared'; $ping = Test-Connection 8.8.8.8 -Count 1 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if ($ping) { Write-Output 'Internet: Connected' } else { Write-Output 'Internet: Still not working - contact IT' }

Actions.Name: Check Network Status
Actions.Description: Test your internet connection
Actions.Script: $ping = Test-Connection 8.8.8.8 -Count 2 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if ($ping) { $ms = [math]::Round(($ping | Measure-Object ResponseTime -Average).Average); Write-Output "Internet: Working (${ms}ms)" } else { Write-Output 'Internet: NOT CONNECTED' }; Write-Output ''; Get-NetIPAddress -AddressFamily IPv4 | Where-Object { $_.IPAddress -ne '127.0.0.1' } | Select-Object -First 1 | ForEach-Object { Write-Output "Your IP: $($_.IPAddress)" }

Actions.Name: Renew IP Address
Actions.Description: Get a new IP address from DHCP
Actions.Script: ipconfig /release | Out-Null; Start-Sleep 2; ipconfig /renew | Out-Null; Start-Sleep 3; Write-Output 'IP address renewed'; Get-NetIPAddress -AddressFamily IPv4 | Where-Object { $_.IPAddress -ne '127.0.0.1' } | Select-Object -First 1 | ForEach-Object { Write-Output "New IP: $($_.IPAddress)" }

Actions.Name: Check Printer Status
Actions.Description: See if print service is running
Actions.Script: $s = Get-Service Spooler; Write-Output "Print Service: $($s.Status)"; Write-Output ''; Write-Output 'Your printers:'; Get-Printer | Select-Object -First 5 | ForEach-Object { Write-Output "- $($_.Name)" }

Actions.Name: Fix Print Spooler
Actions.Description: Restart print service to fix stuck jobs
Actions.Script: Stop-Service Spooler -Force; Start-Sleep 2; Remove-Item "$env:SystemRoot\System32\spool\PRINTERS\*" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Start-Service Spooler; Start-Sleep 2; Write-Output "Print Service: $((Get-Service Spooler).Status)"; Write-Output 'Print queue cleared'

Actions.Name: Clear Print Queue
Actions.Description: Remove stuck print jobs
Actions.Script: Get-PrintJob | Remove-PrintJob -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Write-Output 'Print queue cleared'

Actions.Name: Check Why PC is Slow
Actions.Description: See what's using your resources
Actions.Script: $os = Get-CimInstance Win32_OperatingSystem; $mem = [math]::Round(($os.TotalVisibleMemorySize - $os.FreePhysicalMemory) / $os.TotalVisibleMemorySize * 100); Write-Output "Memory Usage: ${mem}%"; if ($mem -gt 80) { Write-Output '⚠ Memory is high!' }; Write-Output ''; Write-Output 'Top memory users:'; Get-Process | Sort-Object WorkingSet64 -Descending | Select-Object -First 5 | ForEach-Object { Write-Output "- $($_.ProcessName): $([math]::Round($_.WorkingSet64/1MB))MB" }

Actions.Name: Clear Temporary Files
Actions.Description: Free up disk space by clearing temp files
Actions.Script: $before = [math]::Round((Get-ChildItem $env:TEMP -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object Length -Sum).Sum/1MB,1); Remove-Item "$env:TEMP\*" -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; $after = [math]::Round((Get-ChildItem $env:TEMP -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object Length -Sum).Sum/1MB,1); Write-Output "Freed: $([math]::Round($before-$after,1)) MB"

Actions.Name: Check Disk Space
Actions.Description: See how much space you have left
Actions.Script: $d = Get-PSDrive C; $free = [math]::Round($d.Free/1GB,1); $total = [math]::Round(($d.Used+$d.Free)/1GB,1); $pct = [math]::Round($d.Free/($d.Used+$d.Free)*100); Write-Output "Disk Space: ${free}GB free of ${total}GB"; if ($pct -lt 10) { Write-Output '⚠ Disk space is low!' } else { Write-Output '✓ Disk space OK' }

Actions.Name: Refresh Desktop
Actions.Description: Fix frozen taskbar or desktop icons
Actions.Script: Stop-Process -Name explorer -Force; Start-Sleep 2; Start-Process explorer; Write-Output 'Desktop refreshed'

Actions.Name: Fix Microsoft Teams
Actions.Description: Clear Teams cache to fix issues
Actions.Script: $teams = Get-Process Teams -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if ($teams) { Stop-Process -Name Teams -Force; Start-Sleep 2 }; Remove-Item "$env:APPDATA\Microsoft\Teams\Cache\*" -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Remove-Item "$env:APPDATA\Microsoft\Teams\blob_storage\*" -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Write-Output 'Teams cache cleared'; Write-Output 'Please restart Teams'

Actions.Name: Fix Outlook Slowness
Actions.Description: Clear Outlook autocomplete cache
Actions.Script: $outlook = Get-Process OUTLOOK -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if ($outlook) { Write-Output 'Please close Outlook first' } else { Remove-Item "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Outlook\RoamCache\*" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Write-Output 'Outlook cache cleared' }

Actions.Name: Clear Browser Cache
Actions.Description: Clear Edge browser cache
Actions.Script: $edge = Get-Process msedge -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if ($edge) { Write-Output 'Please close Edge browser first' } else { Remove-Item "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\Default\Cache\*" -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Write-Output 'Edge cache cleared' }

Actions.Name: My Computer Info
Actions.Description: See your computer name and user
Actions.Script: Write-Output "Computer: $env:COMPUTERNAME"; Write-Output "User: $env:USERNAME"; Write-Output "Domain: $env:USERDOMAIN"

Actions.Name: Time Since Restart
Actions.Description: Check when your PC was last restarted
Actions.Script: $boot = (Get-CimInstance Win32_OperatingSystem).LastBootUpTime; $up = (Get-Date) - $boot; Write-Output "Last restart: $($boot.ToString('MMM dd, yyyy HH:mm'))"; Write-Output "Running for: $($up.Days) days, $($up.Hours) hours"; if ($up.Days -gt 7) { Write-Output ''; Write-Output '⚠ Consider restarting your PC' }

Actions.Name: Check for IT Support
Actions.Description: Get info to share with IT helpdesk
Actions.Script: Write-Output '=== Info for IT Support ==='; Write-Output "Computer: $env:COMPUTERNAME"; Write-Output "User: $env:USERDOMAIN\$env:USERNAME"; $os = Get-CimInstance Win32_OperatingSystem; Write-Output "OS: $($os.Caption)"; Write-Output "Build: $($os.BuildNumber)"; Get-NetIPAddress -AddressFamily IPv4 | Where-Object { $_.IPAddress -ne '127.0.0.1' } | Select-Object -First 1 | ForEach-Object { Write-Output "IP: $($_.IPAddress)" }

Actions.Name: Check SCCM Status
Actions.Description: See if SCCM client is running
Actions.Script: $s = Get-Service CcmExec -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if ($s) { Write-Output "SCCM Service: $($s.Status)"; Write-Output "Start Type: $($s.StartType)" } else { Write-Output 'SCCM Client: Not installed' }

Actions.Name: Check SCCM Cache
Actions.Description: See SCCM cache folder size
Actions.Script: $path = 'C:\Windows\ccmcache'; if (Test-Path $path) { $size = [math]::Round((Get-ChildItem $path -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object Length -Sum).Sum/1MB,1); Write-Output "SCCM Cache: ${size} MB" } else { Write-Output 'SCCM cache folder not found' }

Actions.Name: Check Recent SCCM Logs
Actions.Description: See latest SCCM log activity
Actions.Script: $logPath = 'C:\Windows\CCM\Logs'; if (Test-Path $logPath) { Get-ChildItem $logPath -Filter '*.log' | Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 5 | ForEach-Object { Write-Output "$($_.Name): $($_.LastWriteTime.ToString('MM/dd HH:mm'))" } } else { Write-Output 'SCCM logs not found' }

Actions.Name: Restart SCCM Service
Actions.Description: Restart the SCCM client service
Actions.Script: $s = Get-Service CcmExec -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if ($s) { Restart-Service CcmExec -Force; Start-Sleep 3; Write-Output "SCCM Service: $((Get-Service CcmExec).Status)" } else { Write-Output 'SCCM not installed' }
